name: Javanese Ngoko
method: Sound-first, story-led, no-translation-first recall. Goal: warm casual Javanese for daily connection in Indonesia.

days.title: Day 1 — Aku, kowe, saiki
days.story: Narrative (sound-first): Narrative (sound-first): Narrative (sound-first): Aku Raditya. Aku saiki sinau basa Jawa. Aku ora perlu sampurna. Aku mung perlu wani ngomong sethithik saben dina. Dina iki aku miwiti saka ukara cilik sing bisa tak gunakake karo wong liya. Today’s goal is not vocabulary volume. It is confident output. You will (1) listen/imitate, (2) speak in short bursts, (3) read for meaning, (4) write a small output, and (5) recall without looking. This matches the First 20 Hours principle: target performance, deconstruct, practice hard parts first, get feedback, repeat. Micro-story method: read once for meaning, then speak the story sentence-by-sentence. If you get stuck, do not translate everything—use context, then check one key word. The win condition is: you can retell the story in your own words using the same sentence pattern. Output target (today): you can say 8–10 sentences clearly, with correct rhythm, without rushing. Then you can write 3–5 lines that reuse the pattern but change one variable (time, place, feeling, purpose). Recall target (later): after 10 minutes, recall 3 sentences without looking; after 3 hours, recall again; tomorrow morning, recall the anchor sentence once. This is how your brain locks it in.
